verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When Jesus had thus spoken, He spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and He anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay, and said, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those who intend to gain any advantage from what they read, must not pass by even any small portion of the words; and on this account we are bidden to “search” the Scriptures, because most of the words, although at first sight easy, appear to have in their depth much hidden meaning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For observe of what sort is the present case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Having said these words,” It saith,“He spat on the ground.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That the glory of God should be made manifest,” and that, “I must work the works of Him that sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For not without a cause hath the Evangelist mentioned to us His words, and added that, “He spat,” but to show that He confirmed His words by deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why used He not water instead of spittle for the clay?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was about to send the man to Siloam: in order therefore that nothing might be ascribed to the fountain, but that thou mightest learn that the power proceeding from His mouth, the same both formed and opened the man’s eyes, He “spat on the ground”; this at least the Evangelist signified, when he said, “And made clay of the spittle.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, that the successful issue might not seem to be of the earth, He bade him wash.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore did He not this at once, instead of sending him to Siloam?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn the faith of the blind man, and that the obstinacy of the Jews might be silenced: for it was probable that they would all see him as he departed, having the clay spread upon his eyes, since by the strangeness of the thing he would attract to himself all, both those who did and those who did not know him, and they would observe him exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And because it is not easy to recognize a blind man who hath recovered sight, He first maketh by the length of way many to be witnesses, and by the strangeness of the spectacle exact observers, that being more attentive they may no longer be able to say, “It is he: it is not he.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, by sending him to Siloam, He desireth to prove that He is not estranged from the Law and the Old (Covenant), nor could it afterwards be feared that Siloam would receive the glory, since many who had often washed their eyes there gained no such benefit; for there also it was the power of Christ that wrought all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account the Evangelist addeth for us the interpretation of the name; for having said, “in Siloam,” he addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn that there also it was Christ who healed him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As Paul saith, “They drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them, and that Rock was Christ.” ( 1 Cor. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then Christ was the spiritual Rock, so also was He the spiritual Siloam.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To me also the sudden coming in of the water seems to hint an ineffable mystery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The unlooked for (nature) of His appearance, beyond all expectation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe the mind of the blind man, obedient in everything.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “If it is really the clay or the spittle which gives me eyes, what need of Siloam?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if there be need of Siloam, what need of the clay?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did he anoint me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why bid me wash?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he entertained no such thoughts, he held himself prepared for one thing only, to obey in all things Him who gave the command, and nothing that was done offended him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any one ask, “How then did he recover his sight, when he had removed the clay?” he will hear no other answer from us than that we know not the manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what wonder if we know it not, since not even the Evangelist knew, nor the very man that was healed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What had been done he knew, but the manner of doing it he could not comprehend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So when he was asked he said, that “He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see”; but how this took place he cannot tell them, though they ask ten thousand times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The neighbors therefore, and they which had seen him, that he was a beggar, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some said, This is he.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The strangeness of what had been brought to pass led them even to unbelief, though so much had been contrived that they might not disbelieve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They said, “Is not this he that sat and begged?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O the lovingkindness of God!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whither did He descend, when with great kindness He healed even beggars, and so silenced the Jews, because He deemed not the illustrious, nor the distinguished, nor the rulers, but men of no mark to be fit objects of the same Providence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He came for the salvation of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what happened in the case of the paralytic, happened also with this man, for neither did the one or the other know who it was that healed him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this was caused by the retirement of Christ, for Jesus when He healed always retired, that all suspicion might be removed from the miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since how could they who knew not who He was flatter Him, or join in contriving what had been done?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Neither was this man one of those who went about, but of those who sat at the doors of the Temple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now when all were doubting concerning him, what saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was not ashamed of his former blindness, nor did he fear the wrath of the people, nor did he decline showing himself that he might proclaim his Benefactor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They said unto him, How were thine eyes opened?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Doth “a man” work such deeds?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As yet he knew nothing great concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how truthful he is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith not whence He made it, for he speaks not of what he doth not know; he saw not that He spat on the ground, but that He spread it on he knew from sense and touch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And said unto me, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This too his hearing witnessed to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how did he recognize His voice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From His conversation with the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And saying all this, and having received the witness by the works, the manner (of the cure) he cannot tell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if faith is needed in matters which are felt and handled, much more in the case of things invisible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They said unto him, Where is he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said, I know not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They said, “Where is he?” having already murderous intentions against Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe the modesty of Christ, how He continued not with those who were healed; because He neither desired to reap glory, nor to draw a multitude, nor to make a show of Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe too how truthfully the blind man maketh all his answers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews desired to find Christ to bring Him to the priests, but when they did not find Him, they brought the blind man to the Pharisees, as to those who would question him more severely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For which reason the Evangelist remarks, that it was “the Sabbath” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14 ), in order to point out their wicked thoughts, and the cause for which they sought Him, as though forsooth they had found a handle, and could disparage the miracle by means of what appeared to be a transgression of the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is clear from their saying immediately on seeing him nothing but, “How opened he thine eyes?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe also the manner of their speech; they say not, “How didst thou receive thy sight?” but, “How opened he thine eyes?” thus affording him an excuse for slandering Jesus, because of His having worked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he speaks to them shortly, as to men who had already heard; for without mentioning His name, or that “He said unto me, Go, wash,” he at once saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He put clay upon my eyes, and I washed, and do see.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the slander was now become great, and the Jews had said, “Behold what work Jesus doth on the Sabbath day, he anointeth with clay!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe, I pray you, how the blind man is not disturbed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When being questioned he spake in the presence of those others without danger, it was no such great thing to tell the truth, but the wonder is, that now when he is placed in a situation of greater fear, he neither denies nor contradicts what he had said before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then did the Pharisees, or rather what did the others also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They had brought him (to the Pharisees), as being about to deny; but, on the contrary, that befell them which they desired not, and they learned more exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this they everywhere have to endure, in the case of miracles; but this point we will more clearly demonstrate in what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What said the Pharisees?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Some said,” (not all, but the more forward,) “This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the Sabbath day; others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that they were led up by the miracles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For hear what they say now, who before this had sent to bring Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if all did not so, (for being rulers through vainglory they fell into unbelief,) yet still the greater number even of the rulers believed on Him, but confessed Him not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the multitude was easily overlooked, as being of no great account in their synagogue, but the rulers being more conspicuous had the greater difficulty in speaking boldly, or some the love of rule restrained, others cowardice, and the fear of the many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also He said, “How can ye believe who receive honor from men?” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And these who were seeking to kill Him unjustly said that they were of God, but that He who healed the blind could not be of God, because He kept not the Sabbath; to which the others objected, that a sinner could not do such miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those first maliciously keeping silence about what had taken place, brought forward the seeming transgression; for they said not, “He healeth on the Sabbath day,” but, “He keepeth not the Sabbath.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These, on the other hand, replied weakly, for when they ought to have shown that the Sabbath was not broken, they rely only upon the miracles; and with reason, for they still thought that He was a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If this had not been the case, they might besides have urged in His defense, that He was Lord of the Sabbath which Himself had made, but as yet they had not this opinion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Anyhow, none of them dared to say what he wished openly, or in the way of an assertion, but only in the way of doubt, some from not having boldness of speech, others through love of rule.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There was therefore a division among them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This division first began among the people, then later among the rulers also, and some said, “He is a good man”; others, “Nay, but he deceiveth the people.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the rulers were more void of understanding than the many, since they were divided later than they? and after they were divided, they did not exhibit any noble feeling, when they saw the Pharisees pressing upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since had they been entirely separated from them, they would soon have known the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is possible to do well in separating.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also Himself hath said, “I am come not to bring peace upon the earth but a sword.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there is an evil concord, and there is a good disagreement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus they who built the tower ( Gen. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4 ), agreed together to their own hurt; and these same again were separated, though unwillingly, yet for their good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus also Corah and his company agreed together for evil, therefore they were separated for good; and Judas agreed with the Jews for evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So division may be good, and agreement may be evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore It saith, “If thine eye offend thee, smite it out, if thy foot, cut it off.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29, and xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if we must separate ourselves from an ill-joined limb, must we not much more from friends united to us for evil ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that agreement is not in all cases a good, just as division is not in all cases an evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things I say, that we may shun wicked men, and follow the good; for if in the case of our limbs we cut off that which is rotten and incurable, fearing lest the rest of the body should catch the same disease, and if we do this not as having no care for that part, but rather as desiring to preserve the remainder, how much more must we do this in the case of those who consent with us for evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we can set them right without receiving injury ourselves, we ought to use every means to do so; but if they remain incorrigible and may injure us, it is necessary to cut them off and cast them away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so they will often be gainers rather (than losers).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also Paul exhorted, saying, “And ye shall put away from among yourselves that wicked person”; and, “that he that hath done this deed may be put away from among you.” ( 1 Cor. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A dreadful thing, dreadful indeed, is the society of wicked men; not so quickly doth the pestilence seize or the itch infect those that come in contact with such as are under the disease, as doth the wickedness of evil men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “evil communications corrupt good manners.” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again the Prophet saith, “Come out from among them, and be ye separate.” ( Isa. lii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let no one then have a wicked man for his friend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if when we have bad sons we publicly disclaim them, without regarding nature or its laws, or the constraint which it lays upon us, much more ought we to fly from our companions and acquaintances when they are wicked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because even if we receive no injury from them, we shall anyhow not be able to escape ill report, for strangers search not into our lives, but judge us from our companions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This advice I address to young men and maidens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Providing,” It saith, “things honest,” not only in the sight of the Lord, but also “in the sight of all men.” ( Rom. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then use every means that our neighbor be not offended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a life, though it be very upright, if it offend others hath lost all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how is it possible for the life that is upright to offend?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When the society of those that are not upright invests it with an evil reputation; for when, trusting in ourselves, we consort with bad men, even though we be not harmed, we offend others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things I say to men and women and maidens, leaving it to their conscience to see exactly how many evils are produced from this source.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Neither I, perhaps, nor any of the more perfect, suspect any ill; but the simpler brother is harmed by occasion of thy perfection; and thou oughtest to be careful also for his infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even if he receive no injury, yet the Greek is harmed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now Paul biddeth us be “without offense, both to Jews and Greeks, and to the Church of God.” ( 1 Cor. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (I think no evil of the virgin, for I love virginity, and “love thinketh no evil” ( 1 Cor. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ); I am a great admirer of that state of life, and I cannot have so much as an unseemly thought about it.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How shall we per suade those that are without?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we must take forethought for them also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then so order what relates to ourselves, that none of the unbelievers may be able even to find a just handle of accusation against us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as they who show forth a right life glorify God, so they who do the contrary cause Him to be blasphemed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: May no such persons be among us: but may our works so shine, that our Father which is in Heaven may be glorified, and that we may enjoy the honor which is from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To which may we all attain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory forever and ever.
